title: Protocol: phase transition in African research ecosystems -- critical density for self-sustaining trial capacity
body: This cross-sectional registry analysis will determine whether a critical per-capita trial density exists at which African research ecosystems transition from dependent to self-sustaining states, inspired by phase-transition models from statistical physics. We will query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for 20 African countries spanning the full density spectrum, collecting four dimensions per country: total interventional trials, condition-specific counts across 12 disease categories using query.cond, local sponsor proxy using query.spons matching country name, and Phase 1 trial counts using AREA[Phase] filtering. Shannon entropy across condition categories will quantify portfolio diversity, normalised as Pielou evenness. A composite sovereignty score will weight local sponsorship at 40 percent, condition evenness at 30 percent, Phase 1 presence at 20 percent, and density at 10 percent. Countries will be classified into four phase states: self-sustaining, transitional, dependent, and pre-critical, based on density and sovereignty thresholds. Limitations include proxy-based sovereignty measurement, single-registry dependence, and the ecological analogy requiring validation through longitudinal data.
ai_transparency: LLM used for drafting; author reviewed and takes responsibility for final content.

checks.name: methods before outcomes
checks.detail: API query and multi-dimensional methods precede classification scheme.

checks.name: limitations stated
checks.detail: Final sentence addresses proxy measurement, single registry, and analogy limitations.
